observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officers:
a) Determine and address all the reasons for the delay in the submission of the
required reports and consider imposing sanctions on the persons responsible for the
delayed submission of the financial reports and supporting documents to the COA
Office. Further, review and evaluate the performance of the finance personnel; and
recommend replacement for those found undesirable with qualified personnel with
the right work attitude;
b) Conduct planning, target setting and prepare commitment reports particularly on
the timelines on the submission of transaction documents/reports and financial
statements provided in Sections 107 and 122 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006, and Sections 59 and 60, Chapter 19, Volume I of the GAM
for NGAs, and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code to avoid repercussions stated
herein; and
c) Conduct periodic monitoring of submission of required reports to COA,
Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see
to it that all the required reports are submitted on time.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to
strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and
supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality
within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from the
receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which
shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly
submitted to the Auditor within ten days after the end of each month together with
the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal
entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Management to require:
3.1.1 The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of the
inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and whereabouts, and
come up with the RPCI and RPCPPE;
3.2.1 The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first
and second semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively, and the
annual RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to the Audit Team for
subsequent review;
3.3.1 The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its system of
maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs, SLCs, PPELCs,
RPCI and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of these records to ensure
reliability of the inventories and PPE account balances as presented in the Financial
Statements of the Municipality.
3.4.1 The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for
the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for
each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of
the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of
accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which
should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are
necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts which its corresponding
Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

Recommendation:
4.1.1 We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies
of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the
funds were utilized within the specified purpose.
4.2.1 We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies
of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the
funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to furnish the Auditor copy of the POs and contract documents
not later than five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of contracts together
with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to
submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning of the year; and
prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in
compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should:
7.1. Formulate a policy on the granting of FA systematically determine the extent of
aid to be given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be properly screened
giving priority to the less privileged constituents.
7.2 Coordinate with the MSWO to assess request for medical and hospitalization
assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents
can be assisted and served.
7.3 Instruct the MSWO to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of
supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment
and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with
the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
8.1 Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review
and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to
the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No.
2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
8.2 Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG
reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

Recommendation
Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the
utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or
capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain
from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the
personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget
Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress
account thru the maintenance of subsidi
ary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions of the
MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify
completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory
Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Lantawan for proper
reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of
depreciation for depreciable assets.

Recommendation:
Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance
with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons
liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and
Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common
Government Transaction must be properly observed.
We recommended further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and monitoring/tracking of the utilization and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary as required.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The failure to submit the Bank Statement was due to the Landbank of the Philippines’ delay in releasing the Bank Statement of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count all inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Rest assured that our office will comply with the above audit recommendation given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Lantawan for proper reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable assets.
observations.reason: Our office will provide all necessary documents to help the audit team to validate the projects mentioned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials concerned shall submit the aforementioned reports as required by the above laws and regulations to this Office as well as making available for audit verification.
observations.reason: Rest assured that we will provide all the above reports and documents in timely manner.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Chief Accountant to: 1. Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these accounts. 2. Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality. 3. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.
observations.reason: Rest assured that we will provide all necessary reports and documents in timely manner.

observations.recommendation: LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly monitoring reports for all on going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.
observations.reason: Rest assured that we will provide all reports and documents in timely manner.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10) days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows: a. Submit the annual LDRRMFIP for 2020 to support the legality of the 2020 DRRM transaction, and b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2021 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2021 transaction.

a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget. I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year. b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes.
observations.reason: Rest assured that we are doing everything we can to avoid more unnecessary delays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160,
RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget
and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and
verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in
the financial statements.

a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical
count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of
physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate
inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not
contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize
unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties
in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in
accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which
prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.

c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure
that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each
PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of
the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of
accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year
which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts.
These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its
corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget
Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress
account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records
consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the
Engineering Division to identify completed projects and request submission of
Property Completion and Inventory Report for all completed projects of the
Municipality of Lantawan for proper reclassification and recording to its appropriate
asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable assets.

Direct the Chief Accountant to:
1. Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the
Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these
accounts.
2. Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General
Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division
to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.
3. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly
monitoring reports for all on going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of
government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10)
days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance
with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete
with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption. It is then imperative that the
agency should comply on the rules and regulations on fuel consumption and keep its
record in detail, as may be necessary, so that the correct and accurate information will
be provided.

We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each
fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully
adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan
(LDRRMFIP) for 2020 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support
the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2021 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2021 transaction.

a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the certified
statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July 15 of the
current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the
Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or
certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with
the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for
the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations
on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction
must be properly observed.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: “The failure to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement was due to the Landbank of the Philippines’ delay in releasing the Bank Statement of the Municipality.”

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Agency to instruct the Local Treasurer/Cashier/Collecting Officer to deposit intact all the collections turned over to them by collectors/tellers, with the authorized depository bank (AGDB) daily or not later than the next banking day.

a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Due to the present situation (Pandemic) majority of the employees are work from home thus resulting to the Municipality’s failure to conduct the inventory count for CY 2020. Rest assured that we will comply with all the audit recommendations given once there are enough manpower and opportunity to conduct the same, following safety guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Lantawan for proper reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable assets.

We recommended that the officials concerned shall submit the aforementioned reports as required by the Commission on Audit and other laws and regulations to this Office as well as making available for audit verification.
observations.reason: Thus, timely audit verification thereof could not be made.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Chief Accountant to:
1.Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these accounts.
2.Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.
3.Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.
observations.managementAction: “The Municipality is currently on the process in making all the necessary ledgers, the delay was due to the limited access in the workplace because majority of the employees are work from home. Rest assured that we will do our best to submit the said ledgers as fast as we can.”

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.
observations.managementAction: “Management assured us that all necessary actions will be complied regarding the opening of a separate depository account for LDRMM, we are just waiting for the approved Resolution by the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing the LCE and the Municipal Treasurer to open an account for the said purpose.”

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2020 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2020 transaction.
observations.managementAction: “We are currently on the process of completing the LDRMMFIP as it is hard to do it in a timely manner because most of the concern personnel are work from home and the Municipality is experiencing poor internet/signal connection. Due to the urgency of this plan, all concern personnel are notified and advised to complete such.”

“As we have emphasized in the last year’s management’s comment regarding this matter, we have informed your office that we will be able to submit the said budget, but unfortunately, due to the pandemic, and since majority of the employees are work from home, there is still a delay in complying the submission of said budget. But rest assured that we are doing everything we can tto avoid more unnecessary delays.”

observations.recommendation: Instruct the HRMO- designate to ensure the preparation of HR Plan composed of those enumerated Job Order Contracts under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all its contracts of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, to which specific employees are detailed which should be likewise attached to the contracts.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/pr emiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation. The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, and all other obligations should take priority over salaries and wages of its employees.

We recommended the following
a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the
certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July
15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current
year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.

observations.reason: The Land Bank of the Philippines refuses to provide for the Monthly Bank Statements of the Municipality, incurs a delay in the submission of its screenshot in lieu of the statements. . The Municipal Accountant will submit the BRS when the Landbank of the Philippines provides the complete monthly bank statements or its screenshot.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Head of the Agency to instruct the Local Treasurer/ Cashier/ Collecting Officer to deposit intact all the collections turned over to them with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in R.A. No. 10121 in setting aside of at least 5% DRRM fund from its estimated revenue from regular sources;
b. Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.
The Municipal Government failed to open a separate depository account separate depository account exclusively for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund in accordance with Section 310 of RA 7160 and Section 3 of COA Circular 92 - 382.
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.
The unexpended / unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for the year end 2018 was not transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account "Trust Liability- DRRM" in the Trust Fund books in violation of COA Circular 2012 - 002 dated September 12, 2012, Section 5.1.10.
We recommended that at the end of each year the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the special trust fund under the account "Trust Liability - DRRM".

We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer submit the list of all on – going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

We recommended the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of R.A. 9003 within their respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12 and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommended the agency to establish Local Council for the Protection of Children composing of chosen responsible members of the community including a representative from the youth sector, as well as representatives from government and private agencies concerned with the welfare of children.

We recommended the following
a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contracts of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, specific instructions given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which should be likewise attached to the contracts.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation. The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.
observations.reason: UPDATED AMOUNT AS SUBMITTED BY GSIS Z,AMBOANGA P88,996,167.06 We are also requesting the Accountant you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record. (See Annex-A for Schedule )

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board to submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate
review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts
in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Head of the Agency to instruct the Local Treasurer/
Cashier/ Collecting Officer to deposit intact all the collections turned over to them
with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count
inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count
and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be
prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the
Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of
these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance
with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the
maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that
the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account
to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of
Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the
appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be
charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in
order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated
Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to
locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the
maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the
provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division
to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and
Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Lantawan for proper
reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of
depreciation for depreciable assets.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository
account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall
faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan
(LDRRMFIP) for 2018 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to
support the legality of the 2018 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2018 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2017
transaction.

Recommendation:
We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer
submit the list of all on – going government projects/programs/activities and those
that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s
report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for
the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of R.A. 9003 within their
respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12
and Section 16 of the same Act.

Recommendation:
In recognizing the right of children to assistance, including proper care and
nutrition, and special protection from all forms of neglect, abuse, cruelty and
exploitation, and other conditions prejudicial to their development, we recommended
the Municipal Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by
appropriating each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for the
operation of the LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the fund allocated for the
protection of children.

Recommendation:

a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the
certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July
15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the
Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or
certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes not later than
seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law. We recommended that at the end of each year the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM officer shall transfer all the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”. a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2018 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2018 DRRM transaction, and b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2017 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2017 transaction.

In recognizing the right of children to assistance, including proper care and nutrition, and special protection from all forms of neglect, abuse, cruelty and exploitation, and other conditions prejudicial to their development, we recommended the Municipal Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by appropriating each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for the operation of the LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the fund allocated for the protection of children.

We recommended the following
a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.

b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes not later than seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF budget.

We recommended the agency to comply with the provision and guidelines stipulated in the DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 - 001 dated February 22, 2017. The appropriation and utilization of the twenty percent (20%) DF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development & environment management outcomes of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure”

We recommended the Audited agency to appropriate 5% of the total budget appropriation of the agency and shall submit a copy of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the COA Audit Team assigned to the agency within five (5) working days from the received of the approved plan from the PCW or their mother or central offices. Likewise, a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished the said Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contracts of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which should be likewise attached to the contracts.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation. The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees. We are also requesting the Accountant you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record. (See Annex-A for Schedule )

Maximize the application of the LGU’s power to levy real property tax pursuant to Sections 232 and 233, Chapter 4 of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160.

Impose Special Levy on Real Property in accordance with Section 235 of Chapter 5, R.A. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991 which should be 1% on the assessed value of real property, which is   same with the basic tax.

The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and 
Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contracts of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which should be likewise attached to the contracts.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular that only allowable expenses are charges to the fund.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submits same to the Office of the Auditor

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic Act 9003 particularly on waste segregation and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazards.

We recommend that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

We recommended that the Local School Board to submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.
We are also requesting the Accountant    you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.  (See Annex-A   for Schedule )
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations. (See annex-B for Schedule)

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule)
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

Properly observe COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 the prescribed deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts. Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts. Reconcile the Inventory Report with the Property Cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.

We recommend that the agency submit copies of the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as mandated under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Together with the bidding, BAC resolution minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic Act 9003 particularly on waste segregation  and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazards.

Recommendation:
We recommend that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board to submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

Recommendation:
The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

Recommendation
The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.
We  are also requesting the Accountant    you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.  (See Annex-A   for Schedule )

Recommendation
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations. (See annex-B for Schedule)

Recommendation
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule)

Recommendation
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

Recommendation:
Properly observe COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 the prescribed deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Recommendation:
Ensure that identified PPAs address emerging gender-related issues and concerns pursuant to PCW DILG DNM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01;
Utilization of GAD Budget Should be in accordance with the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
Submit monthly accomplishment report to our office.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sangguaniang Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan.  Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.

Recommendation:
Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported.

Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.

Management should require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate special accounts in the General Fund for the Market Operations and Slaughterhouse Operations with the respective set of subsidiary ledgers likewise, the post-closing trial balance and related financial statements for each special account at the end of the year should be prepared.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.

We also requesting   you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875.

a)	The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438).
b)	Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) be maintained.
c)	The Municipal Budget Officer maintain record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.
